description: Synthetic embedding triplets for grounding-faithfulness tests (DRIFT-24). Uses standard-basis vectors (or near-basis) so cosine similarity is analytically exact. 4-dim for simplicity.

examples.label: lazy
examples.description: Response ≈ query direction [1,0,0,0]; context is orthogonal [0,1,0,0]. cos(resp,ctx)~0 < groundingThreshold; cos(resp,query)~1 >= lazyThreshold.

examples.label: drifted
examples.description: Response is orthogonal to both query and context. cos(resp,ctx)=0, cos(resp,query)=0 — both below their thresholds.

examples.label: grounded
examples.description: Response tightly aligned with context; query is orthogonal.

examples.label: lazy
examples.description: Response parrots query direction [0,0,1,0]; context is in opposite quadrant [0,0,-1,0] — cos(resp,ctx)=-1, far below groundingThreshold; cos(resp,query)=1 >= lazyThreshold.
